search_queries_used: site:who.int intimate partner violence clinical handbook first-line support documentation history examination
site:rcem.ac.uk domestic abuse recognition disclosure record guideline
site:dha.gov.ae domestic violence healthcare guideline UAE
candidate_sources_rejected: screening field treated as disclosure or confirmed abuse
automatic safeguarding, police, legal, social-service, or emergency referral
intimate examination or injury findings inferred from the workflow
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: A generic documentation workflow cannot establish violence or disclosure.
Actions depend on consent, immediate safety, legal duties, age, capacity, and local UAE policy.
Examination must be explicitly performed and documented.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: WHO guidance primarily addresses women subjected to intimate-partner or sexual violence; men, children, adolescents, older people, people with disabilities, trafficking, and other abuse contexts require tailored local policy and evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary-care documentation only when privacy, consent, immediate safety, legal duties, safeguarding, and qualified clinician practice permit.
UAE_applicability: International WHO and UK emergency guidance requires explicit UAE legal, safeguarding, reporting, privacy, referral, and service-pathway review before clinical use.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact WHO first-line-support section supports private, patient-led documentation of immediate emotional, physical, safety, and support needs without inferring violence, coercing disclosure, or generating an action.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports listening, documenting needs and concerns, validating, assessing safety and recording support discussed without asserting abuse or imposing action.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact history and examination section supports clinician-entered history and examination only when disclosure and consent make it appropriate; no intimate examination, assault, or injury finding is inferred.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports respectful, consent-based history and head-to-toe examination documentation only when relevant and performed; sexual-assault care is not generalized to every domestic-violence presentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact recognition section supports clinician awareness of immediate safety, injury, children, vulnerability, and escalation concerns without treating a generic screening field as a positive finding.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician suspicion, confidential disclosure opportunity and direct enquiry only when clinically indicated; it does not support universal screening.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact documentation section supports accurate clinician-entered records, available findings, patient words, consent-sensitive communication, safety discussion, and follow-up without automatic referral or reporting.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.

unresolved_source_gaps: Disclosure status, immediate danger, children, injuries, sexual violence, capacity, consent, privacy, safeguarding, mandatory reporting, local services, referral, and escalation remain unsupported unless explicitly entered.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
